mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly depressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20d returns occur in >65% of observations when FEDFUNDS rises, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC < 0.03 during periods of stable employment.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real personal spending combined with accelerating inflation indicates demand-pull price pressures that force monetary tightening, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3% annually, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output and corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >55% of observations following INDPRO increases, or autocorrelation in returns completely dominates the macro signal.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in housing starts exceeding 5% signals weakening residential investment and reduced construction sector activity, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20d returns occur in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal shows no predictive power when mortgage rates are below 4%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms broad-based labor market strength that supports consumer confidence and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition yields negative returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction effect vanishes when quarterly GDP growth falls below 1%.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated real interest rates during a disinflationary phase reduce corporate borrowing costs relative to nominal rates, supporting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Positive returns dominate less than 50% of observations during disinflation periods, or the signal reverses direction when credit spreads widen by more than 100 basis points.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product reflects underlying economic expansion that boosts corporate earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in >55% of observations following positive GDPC1 revisions, or the signal is fully subsumed by market pricing of GDP surprises within 48 hours.
